# BRAINSTORM.md — signal-studio-auth
*Generated by Brainstorming Agent — Judge v2 Protocol*

## Repo Summary
FastAPI auth proxy for Signal Studio / SignalHaus.AI. Supabase-backed with Redis refresh token rotation, RBAC, dual-mode JWT middleware, org invitation flow, password reset. Well-tested (~3300 LOC). Missing deployment infra, audit logging, MFA, CI/CD.

**Scores:** revenue_potential=6 | strategic_value=8 | completeness=7 | urgency=5 | effort_remaining=7

---

## 1. New Features 🚀

### 1a. MFA / TOTP Support *(High Value, Medium Effort)*
- Add `POST /auth/mfa/enroll` — generate TOTP secret + QR code via Supabase MFA API
- Add `POST /auth/mfa/verify` — verify TOTP code, issue session upgrade token
- Add `POST /auth/mfa/unenroll` — remove MFA from account (admin or self)
- Supabase supports TOTP natively via `/auth/v1/factors` — just needs proxy endpoints
- **Why:** Enterprise customers (ForwardLane's Invesco etc.) require MFA compliance

### 1b. Magic Link / Passwordless Auth *(Medium Value, Low Effort)*
- `POST /auth/magic-link` — send OTP/magic link email via Supabase `/auth/v1/otp`
- `POST /auth/verify-otp` — verify OTP code → issue tokens
- **Why:** Better UX for Signal Studio end users, reduces password management

### 1c. Social OAuth Proxy *(Medium Value, Medium Effort)*
- `GET /auth/oauth/{provider}` — redirect to Supabase OAuth flow (Google, GitHub, LinkedIn)
- `GET /auth/oauth/callback` — handle callback, wrap tokens with opaque refresh
- **Why:** Reduces signup friction for Signal Studio marketing/sales users

### 1d. Session Management API *(High Value, Low Effort)*
- `GET /auth/sessions` — list all active sessions for current user (Redis keys with rt: prefix)
- `DELETE /auth/sessions/{session_id}` — revoke specific session
- `DELETE /auth/sessions` — revoke all sessions (emergency logout)
- **Why:** Security requirement for enterprise clients; enables "sign out everywhere"

### 1e. Organization Role Management *(High Value, Medium Effort)*
- `GET /auth/org/{org_id}/members` — list org members with roles
- `PATCH /auth/org/{org_id}/members/{user_id}/role` — change member role (admin only)
- `DELETE /auth/org/{org_id}/members/{user_id}` — remove member from org
- **Why:** Currently only supports invite; no lifecycle management

### 1f. Webhook Event System *(Medium Value, Medium Effort)*
- Emit events on: login, logout, signup, password change, role change, invitation
- Configurable webhook URL per org (stored in Supabase)
- Signed with HMAC-SHA256 for security
- **Why:** Enables downstream services to react to auth events (audit trail, notifications)

---

## 2. Code Quality 🔧

### 2a. Eliminate Duplicate Rate Limiter Logic *(High Priority, Low Effort)*
- **Problem:** `_build_rate_limiter()` and `_redis_or_memory_check()` are near-identical (lines 64-107 vs 176-210 in auth_routes.py)
- **Fix:** Consolidate into a single `RateLimiter` class with `.check(key)` method, expose `._calls` as property for test compatibility
- Remove `_LimiterShim` wrapper — it's pure overhead

### 2b. Add `response_model` to All Routes *(Medium Priority, Low Effort)*
- `LoginResponse`, `SignupResponse`, `UserResponse` models exist in `models.py` but aren't used as `response_model=` on routes
- Add them → auto-generates OpenAPI schema, validates output shape, strips extra fields

### 2c. Password Strength Validation *(Medium Priority, Low Effort)*
- Current: only `len >= 8` check
- Add: uppercase, lowercase, digit, special char requirements
- Expose as configurable policy (env var: `PASSWORD_MIN_LENGTH`, `PASSWORD_REQUIRE_SPECIAL`)

### 2d. Type Annotations *(Low Priority, Low Effort)*
- `_wrap_with_opaque_token` returns `dict` — should be typed as `dict[str, Any]`
- `_supabase_headers` return type missing
- Add `mypy` strict config

### 2e. Centralize Error Responses *(Medium Priority, Low Effort)*
- DRY violation: HTTP 401/403 raise patterns repeated across routes and middleware
- Create `raise_unauthorized()`, `raise_forbidden()` helpers with consistent error format

---

## 3. Testing 🧪

### 3a. Missing: E2E Tests Against Supabase Test Project *(High Priority, High Effort)*
- Spin up a Supabase test project (free tier) with test credentials
- Run full auth flow: signup → login → refresh → logout
- Use pytest fixtures with real HTTP calls
- Gate in CI with `TEST_SUPABASE_URL` env var

### 3b. Missing: CORS Tests *(Medium Priority, Low Effort)*
- No CORS middleware configured! Test what happens with cross-origin requests
- Add `CORSMiddleware` with configurable allowed origins (env: `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS`)

### 3c. Missing: Middleware Integration Tests *(Medium Priority, Medium Effort)*
- Test `supabase_auth_middleware` with expired tokens, malformed tokens, dual mode
- Test that `request.state.supabase_claims` is populated correctly

### 3d. Missing: Load/Stress Tests *(Low Priority, Medium Effort)*
- Use `locust` to test rate limiter behavior under concurrent load
- Verify Redis-backed limiter correctly blocks across concurrent requests

### 3e. Test Coverage Report *(Low Priority, Low Effort)*
- Add `pytest-cov` to requirements
- Generate coverage HTML report in CI
- Target: >85% line coverage

---

## 4. Integrations 🔌

### 4a. Structured Audit Logging → Supabase / Postgres *(High Priority, Medium Effort)*
- Log every auth event: login (success/fail), signup, logout, password change, invitation, role change
- Schema: `audit_logs(id, event_type, user_id, org_id, ip_address, user_agent, metadata, created_at)`
- Write async (fire-and-forget) to not block request path
- **Why:** Compliance requirement for enterprise; forensics for security incidents

### 4b. Sentry Error Tracking *(High Priority, Low Effort)*
- Add `sentry-sdk[fastapi]` to requirements
- Initialize with `SENTRY_DSN` env var
- Capture unhandled exceptions, Supabase API errors

### 4c. PostHog Analytics *(Medium Priority, Low Effort)*
- Track: signups, logins, failed auth attempts, password resets
- Use server-side PostHog client (no PII — just user_id and event type)
- **Why:** Funnel visibility for Signal Studio growth

### 4d. Redis Health Check *(Medium Priority, Low Effort)*
- Add `/health/redis` endpoint that pings Redis
- Include in main `/health` response as `{"status":"ok","redis":"connected"}`

---

## 5. Workflows ⚙️

### 5a. Dockerfile + docker-compose *(Critical, Low Effort)*
- `Dockerfile` with multi-stage build (base → deps → app)
- `docker-compose.yml` with: app, redis, (optionally local Supabase via `supabase/postgres`)
- `.env.example` with all required env vars documented

### 5b. GitHub Actions CI *(High Priority, Low Effort)*
- Lint: `ruff check .`
- Type check: `mypy .`
- Test: `pytest --cov=. --cov-report=xml`
- Security scan: `bandit -r .` + `safety check`
- Trigger on: push to main, PRs

### 5c. Pre-commit Hooks *(Medium Priority, Low Effort)*
- `.pre-commit-config.yaml` with: ruff, mypy, bandit, trailing-whitespace, end-of-file-fixer
- Ensures code quality gates locally before CI

### 5d. Railway / Fly.io Deployment Config *(High Priority, Low Effort)*
- `railway.json` or `fly.toml` with resource config
- Env var documentation in README
- Health check path: `/health`

---

## 6. Performance ⚡

### 6a. Redis Connection Pooling *(High Priority, Low Effort)*
- Current: `get_redis()` creates a new client instance on each call (or relies on redis-py's internal pool)
- Fix: Create a module-level `redis.ConnectionPool` and share it; expose via `app.state.redis_pool`
- Reduces TCP connection overhead under load

### 6b. JWT Verification Caching *(Medium Priority, Medium Effort)*
- Cache verified JWT claims by token hash (LRU cache, TTL = token expiry - 30s)
- Avoids re-verifying the same token on every request in high-throughput scenarios
- Use `cachetools.TTLCache` (thread-safe)

### 6c. Rate Limiter Initialization *(Low Priority, Low Effort)*
- Rate limiters currently initialize at module load time with Redis connection attempt
- Move to app startup (lifespan) to avoid slow module imports

---

## 7. Security 🔒

### 7a. Add CORS Middleware *(Critical, Low Effort)*
- Currently no CORS configured — any origin can make requests
- Add `CORSMiddleware` with `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` env var (default: deny all)

### 7b. Add Security Headers *(High Priority, Low Effort)*
- `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`
- `X-Frame-Options: DENY`
- `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000`
- `Content-Security-Policy` (for API: `default-src 'none'`)
- Use `starlette-security-headers` or custom middleware

### 7c. Brute Force Protection Enhancement *(High Priority, Medium Effort)*
- Current: 5 login attempts / 60s per IP
- Add: progressive delays (exponential backoff per IP after 3 failures)
- Add: account lockout after N consecutive failures (store in Redis with TTL)
- Add: CAPTCHA bypass token support for trusted clients

### 7d. Dependency Audit *(High Priority, Low Effort)*
- Run `safety check` against requirements.txt
- Pin all deps to exact versions in `requirements.lock`
- Add `pip-audit` to CI

### 7e. Request ID Tracing *(Medium Priority, Low Effort)*
- Add `X-Request-ID` header injection middleware
- Log request ID with every log line
- Return `X-Request-ID` in all responses for client-side correlation
